    1. compare(T, T)

Interface PartialComparator<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the type of objects that may be compared by this comparator
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractDomainLattice, AbstractPredicateAbstractionLattice, BooleanLattice, ConjunctivePredicateAbstractionLattice, DisjunctivePredicateAbstractionLattice, SignAnalysisLattice, SimplePredicateAbstractionLattice
public interface PartialComparator<T>

A comparison function, which imposes a partial ordering on some collection of objects.

The ordering imposed by a comparator c on a set of elements S is said to be consistent with equals if and only if c.compare(e1, e2)==EQ has the same boolean value as e1.equals(e2) for every e1 and e2 in S.

See Also:
  • Comparator

    Interface
    Description

    PartialComparator.PartialComparisonResult
    Possible results of the comparison.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    PartialComparator.PartialComparisonResult
    compare(T o1, T o2)
    Compares its two arguments for order.

  • Method Details

    • compare

      PartialComparator.PartialComparisonResult compare(T o1, T o2)
      Compares its two arguments for order. If the arguments are incomparable, UNDEF is returned. Otherwise, the method returns LTE, EQ, or GTE as the first argument is less than, equal to, or greater than the second.

      Parameters:
      o1 - the first object to be compared.
      o2 - the second object to be compared.
      Returns:
      LTE, EQ, or GTE as the first argument is less than, equal to, or greater than the second; returns UNDEF if the arguments are incomparable.
